basic_details.job_title: UT Administration of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu Recruitment 2026 - 60 Contract Posts via Walk-in-Interview
basic_details.short_information: The Department of Social Welfare, Women and Child Development, UT Administration of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu is recruiting 60 candidates on a short-term contract basis through a Walk-in-Interview scheduled on 23, 24, and 25 June 2026 at 9:30 AM. Posts span three national schemes - Mission Vatsalaya, Mission Shakti, and Mission Saksham Anganwadi & POSHAN 2.0 - across DNH, Daman, and Diu districts. No application fee is charged, and candidates must bring a filled application form along with original documents on the interview day.

qualification.details: Eligibility varies significantly by post. Candidates wondering about the minimum qualification should check the post-specific requirements below. The general age limit is 18 to 35 years; however, candidates with experience in Social Welfare and Women & Child Development may be considered up to 50 years of age. | Senior/Coordinator-Level Posts (DCPO, Protection Officers, Project Coordinator, Gender Specialist, Mission Coordinator) | Post Graduate degree in Social Work, Sociology, Child Development, Human Rights, Public Administration, Psychology, Psychiatry, Law, Public Health, or Community Resource Management from a recognized university. Alternatively, a Graduate in the same disciplines with at least 2 years' experience in project formulation, implementation, monitoring, and supervision in Women & Child Development or Social Welfare. DCPO requires a minimum of 3 years' experience with a Govt./Non-Govt. organization in Documentation, Training & Capacity Building. Proficiency in Computers is required for all such posts. | Legal cum Probation Officer | LLB from a recognized university with at least 2 years' experience working with Govt./NGO in legal matters, preferably in the field of Women & Child Rights. | District Coordinator / Block Coordinator (POSHAN 2.0) | Graduate or Certification/Diploma in Computer Science or IT. At least 2 years' experience in application maintenance and support. Computer literacy, willingness to travel, and good oral/written communication in local language are mandatory. Mandatorily local candidates should be engaged. | Centre Administrator (One Stop Centre) | Any woman with a Master's degree in Law, Social Work, Sociology, or Social Science/Psychology. At least 5 years' experience on women-related domains in a Govt. or Non-Govt. administrative setup. At least 1 year of counselling experience is required. Should preferably be a resident of the local community. | Case Worker (One Stop Centre) | Any woman with a Bachelor's degree in Law, Social Work, Sociology, or Social Science/Psychology. At least 3 years' experience in a Govt. or Non-Govt. project/programme in women-related domains. | Accounts Officer / Accountant / Store Keeper Cum Accountant | Graduate in Commerce or Mathematics from a recognized university. At least 1 year's experience in the desired field with Computer Skills and command on Tally. | Data Entry Operator | 10th class pass under the 10+2 system from any recognized board. | Asst-Cum-Data Entry Operator | 12th pass from a recognized board with a Diploma/Certificate in Computers. | PT Instructor cum Yoga Trainer | Degree/Diploma in Physical Education or Certificate Course in Yoga from a recognized university/institution. | Paramedical Staff | Auxiliary Nurse Midwifery (ANM) / General Nurse Midwifery (GNM) / Diploma or Degree in Pharmacy from a recognized institution/university. | Security Guard / Night Guard | At least 2 years' experience as security personnel in a government or reputed organization. Preference for retired military or para-military personnel. | Ayah | Any literate woman who can take care of children below 5 years of age. | Call Operator (WCD Control Room / Women Helpline) | Good communication skills in Hindi, English, and/or regional languages. Requisite qualifications and experience in telecom/web-based systems. | Helper cum Night Watchman | 7th Standard and above; willing to reside in the institution at night. | Helper for Cleaning and Cooking | Any literate person with knowledge/experience in the relevant domain; High School pass preferred.

salary_details.₹30,000: District Coordinator, Project Coordinator, Centre Administrator
salary_details.₹27,804: Protection Officer (Institutional & Non-Institutional), Legal cum Probation Officer
salary_details.₹23,170: Probation Officer/Child Welfare Officer/Case Worker, Accounts Officer (SCPS)
salary_details.₹20,000: Block Coordinator, Accounts Assistant, Psycho-social Counselor
salary_details.₹18,536: Social Worker (Male), Accountant, Store Keeper Cum Accountant
salary_details.₹15,000: Data Entry Operator, Call Operator (Women Helpline)
salary_details.₹7,944: Ayah, Chowkidar, Helper cum Night Watchman, Helper for Cleaning & Cooking
salary_details.details: Candidates wondering about the monthly remuneration should note that all posts carry a fixed consolidated monthly salary with no grade pay or pay matrix, as these are short-term contract positions.

selection_process: The selection process for DNH & DD Social Welfare Department recruitment 2026 is straightforward - there is no written examination. Candidates are selected purely through a Walk-in-Interview and document verification.

Step 1 - Document Verification: Candidates report at 9:30 AM at the designated venue on their respective interview day. Original documents and one set of self-attested photocopies are verified. Step 2 - Walk-in-Interview: Candidates appear before the selection panel at the Chamber of the Director-cum-Deputy Secretary (SW/WCD)
DNH & DD (or the Diu venue for Diu applicants). Step 3 - Merit/Selection: Based on performance in the interview

eligible candidates are shortlisted. Step 4 - Engagement on Contract: Selected candidates are engaged on a short-term contract basis under the respective scheme. This is a contractual role with no right to regularization in future.
how_to_apply: Download the Application Form from the official website: www.ddd.gov.in
Fill in all details carefully - name, father's name, address, date of birth, gender, caste category, domicile, educational qualifications, and experience.
Paste a recent self-attested passport-size photograph in the designated box on the form.
Tick the applicable district (DNH / Daman / Diu) on the application form.
Gather all required documents (originals + one set of self-attested photocopies) as listed below.
Report to the venue on your interview day at 9:30 AM sharp - Day 1 (23 June 2026), Day 2 (24 June 2026), or Day 3 (25 June 2026) as per the post you are applying for.
Submit the filled application form along with documents at the venue.
important_instructions: Candidates should carefully review the following conditions before attending the interview, as non-compliance may result in rejection at the venue.

Candidates previously terminated from any Government Organization shall not be considered. All educational qualifications (Master's Degree / Degree / Diploma) must be from a recognized University/Institution approved by the Government of India. Domicile Certificate holders of DNH & DD shall be given preference in selection. No TA/DA will be paid for attending the Walk-in-Interview. The actual number of vacancies may vary as per departmental requirement; the place of posting may also be changed at the Department's discretion. The Department reserves the right to cancel or terminate the recruitment process without assigning any reason. The engagement is contractual in nature and carries no right to regularization in future.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for DNH & DD Social Welfare Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no separate last date — the Walk-in-Interview itself is the application process. Candidates must attend on 23, 24, or 25 June 2026 depending on the post, at 9:30 AM with a filled application form.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in this recruitment?
faq.answer: There are 60 total vacancies across 35 different posts under Mission Vatsalaya, Mission Shakti, and Mission Saksham Anganwadi & POSHAN 2.0 schemes.

faq.question: Q3. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No. There is no application fee for any post. Candidates can appear for the Walk-in-Interview free of charge.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for applying?
faq.answer: The desirable age limit is 18 to 35 years. Candidates with experience in the Social Welfare and Women & Child Development sector may be considered up to 50 years of age.

faq.question: Q5. Where is the Walk-in-Interview held?
faq.answer: For DNH and Daman district candidates: O/o The Director cum Deputy Secretary (SW/WCD), District Court Premises, Fort Area, Moti Daman – 396220. For Diu district candidates: O/o The Child Development Project Officer, Near Sai Baba Temple, Naida, Diu.

faq.question: Q6. What documents should I bring to the Walk-in-Interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must bring the duly filled application form, original certificates (educational qualification, experience, domicile if applicable), and one set of self-attested photocopies of all documents along with a passport-size photograph.
